records.ety: [OE. chastisen; chastien + ending -isen + modern -ise, -ize, L. -izare, Gr. -i`zein. See Chasten.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Chastised (chs*tīzd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Chastising.]

defs: To inflict pain upon, by means of stripes, or in any other manner, for the purpose of punishment or reformation; to punish, as with stripes.
quotes: How fine my master is! I am afraid He will chastise me.
I am glad to see the vanity or envy of the canting chemists thus discovered and chastised.

defs: To reduce to order or obedience; to correct or purify; to free from faults or excesses.
quotes: The gay, social sense, by decency chastised.

defs: To criticize (a person) strongly and directly in order to correct behavior.
